change.behavior: object_location questions can opt in to query_diagnostics by setting include_diagnostics=true. Default QA answers are unchanged.

expected_dsg_effect.memory_query_loop: Failed DSG object-location queries expose whether the system lacked detector support candidates or rejected ambiguous support candidates before falling back to a room-level answer.
expected_dsg_effect.query_behavior: GraphTool users can request structured diagnostics without changing the default answer schema used by saved prediction JSONL files.
expected_dsg_effect.risk_control: The diagnostic path reports evidence state only; it does not choose a support when ambiguity remains.

next_experiment.rerun_needed: After an external detector/RGB-D return, rebuild the detector-only observation sequence and predicted graph, then run object_location with include_diagnostics=true for remaining DSG misses.
next_experiment.target: Use query_diagnostics to separate missing-support recall failures from ambiguous-support query failures before adjusting memory storage or detector prompts.

objective: improve DSG query observability after the VLM-only semantic gate has passed

problem.dsg_failure_mode: When detector-only DSG fell back to IN_ROOM, the saved answer did not explain whether support evidence was absent, ambiguous, or bypassed.
problem.previous_behavior: object_location returned the same room-level fallback shape for missing support candidates and ambiguous same-frame support candidates.

verification.full_verify_result: all checks passed; 798 pytest cases passed; evaluation suite 52/52 passed
green_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_spatial_qa.py -k 'object_location or support_fallback or current_location'

green_result.mypy: Success: no issues found in 2 source files

verification.red_test: python -m pytest -q tests/test_spatial_qa.py -k 'diagnostics_reports' failed before implementation with KeyError: query_diagnostics
